news.title: US consumer inflation slows more than expected in June
news.summary:
STORY: U.S. inflation slowed more than expected in June, but the moderation in consumer prices could be short-lived.

:: 3.5%

The Consumer Price Index from the Labor Department, released on Tuesday, increased by 3.5% annually, a sharp pullback from May's figure and less than economists had expected.

But the number largely reflects a retreat in energy prices, which fell more than five-and-a-half percent in June for their largest monthly decline in over six years.

That was due largely to last month's fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Fast-forward to July and oil prices have rebounded - hitting a four-week high on Tuesday amid renewed conflict in the Middle East.

Excluding volatile energy and food components, the CPI increased 2.6% annually in June. 

"We're committed to the two percent inflation goal."

The data came the same day Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh, appearing before Congress, said the central bank had, quote, "no tolerance for persistently elevated inflation."

"We want economic growth to be more broadbased and we want the change in prices, the increase in inflation to be more limited. There are a lot of things that are happening that are outside of our control as you mentioned - conflicts overseas and the rest. We can't have and shouldn't have a direct control on that. But inflation is a choice. We monetary policymakers need to choose lower prices and that's the commitment my colleagues have made."

Investors on Tuesday were still pricing in at least one rate hike from the central bank before the end of the year.

news.title: OpenAI’s Growing Challenges Narrow Its IPO Window
news.summary: OpenAI is in an IPO pickle.  A year ago, the idea of an OpenAI listing would have had Wall Street salivating, with relatively few reservations about its large financial losses or questions about its leadership.  After surviving an attempted ouster two years prior, chief  Sam Altman  had carved OpenAI into his nascent industry’s undisputed king and hitched himself closely to the much wealthier  Microsoft  to bankroll growth.

news.title: Dollar slides as rate hike prospects ease, oil gains moderate
